published_items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude for Teachers, Free for US K-12 Educators
key_facts: Free for verified US K-12 educators who sign up by June 30, 2027
Includes Claude Code and Cowork for autonomous daily task scheduling
FERPA-compliant; no model training on educator conversations
Nine platform integrations at launch: ASSISTments, Brisk Teaching, Canva Education, others
School/district tier with separately negotiated terms in development

published_items.title: China Companion-AI Enforcement: Doubao/Qwen Shut Down, Shanghai Pulls 14,000 Agents
key_facts: Doubao and Qwen companion-agent features disabled July 15, 2026
Shanghai Cyberspace Administration removed >14,000 non-compliant AI agents pre-deadline
Doubao migrates users to Maoxiang (ByteDance compliance-ready app)
Qwen data permanently deleted after October 15, 2026; no migration path
Regulation: CAC Interim Measures for Anthropomorphic AI Interactive Services, effective July 15

published_items.title: White House Launches Gold Eagle AI Cybersecurity Clearinghouse
key_facts: Launched July 14; established under Executive Order 14409 (June 2, 2026)
Managed by Treasury, CISA, DHS, DoD with private sector partners
Uses frontier AI models to scan for and prioritize cybersecurity vulnerabilities
VINTS platform (Vulnerability Information and Coordination Environment) developed with Carnegie Mellon SEI
Accepts third-party vulnerability reports; coordinates patching across government and critical infrastructure

published_items.title: OpenAI Codex Micro Launches: Developer Macro Pad for Codex
key_facts: First OpenAI-branded hardware; launched July 15, 2026
Co-built with Work Louder (Creator Micro 2 chassis)
13 mechanical keys, joystick, rotary encoder, 6 programmable layers
BLE + Wi-Fi connectivity; on-device local inference
Keys map to Codex agent actions (refactor, test generation, review, docs, security scan)
Price unconfirmed; openai.com returned HTTP 403 during run
Separate from OpenAI's consumer AI home speaker (Bloomberg, 2026-07-14) and Jony Ive hardware project
caveats: Primary launch page (openai.com) unreachable during run — price and final specs unconfirmed

published_items.title: Anthropic Commits $10M CAD to Canadian AI Research
key_facts: $10M CAD committed to eight Canadian institutions
Institutes: Mila (Montréal), Vector (Toronto), Amii (Alberta)
Healthcare: CHEO (pediatric AI), CAMH (computational mental health)
Academic: Université Laval (LLM cross-cultural behavior, low-resource languages), U of T, U of Saskatchewan
Mila, Vector, Amii added to Anthropic Startups program with $5,000 minimum API credits per affiliated startup
Additional partnerships planned later in 2026
